body:
Parent: #651

## Motivation and evidence

Learning loops exist as *plans and ledgers*, but learned items do not demonstrably become new capabilities:

- Issues #498/#499: "make sure we can answer top 10 requests from Google Trends" (+ variations in all languages) — after the closing PRs, 60 of 80 generated prompts still return `intent: unknown`, and the learning loop explicitly "adopts nothing".
- Issue #540 / PR #645: the maintainer's audits found amendment application is "answer decoration… nothing ever reads them… the core vision items — amendments that actually change how tasks are solved, the auto-learning loop from failed dreams, real pattern discovery — remain superficial or unimplemented", and his final comment ("I don't see any changes after this comment") went unanswered by the closing session, which only did CI mechanics.
- Issue #558: "actually recompile itself and reattach to the UI" was reduced to human-gated plans that are never executed.
- Issue #364: "periodically attempt to synthesise new seed rules" — the self-improvement API is proposal-only; nothing runs it periodically.

Open issue #656 (E37) defines the *promotion protocol*; this issue owns the **end-to-end adoption loop** that produces promotable material and proves capability growth.

## Requirements

1. Define the adoption contract: a learning cycle takes a frontier item (an unanswered prompt class, e.g. from Google Trends, `intent: unknown` events, or failed benchmark cases), derives candidate knowledge/rules (seed edits, meanings, substitution rules, compiled skills), validates them against generated tests, and emits a **promotion proposal** in the exact shape #656 consumes.
2. Prove capability delta: for every adopted item, a before/after pair must be recorded — the prompt class answered `unknown` before and answers correctly after, in all supported languages. Store pairs in `data/meta/learning-adoption-ledger.lino`.
3. Close the Google Trends gap concretely: drive the existing frontier (issues #498/#499) through the loop until the top-10 trends prompts and their paraphrase matrices answer (target: 80/80 of the existing generated corpus, or each failure recorded with a named blocking gap).
4. Dreaming amendments (issue #540, R413) must be shown to change solving behavior through the production path — extend the existing `learned_amendment_changes_a_new_task_answer_without_repeating_requirement` test into a *class-level* suite (multiple topics, multiple languages), and delete any decoration-only code path found on the way.
5. The loop must run periodically (dreaming runtime and/or scheduled CI) in proposal mode by default, honoring the human gate; with #656 merged, promotion of passing proposals becomes the reviewed PR path.
6. Every failure to adopt is preserved as a durable frontier record (never silently dropped), consistent with R425.

## Acceptance criteria

- `formal-ai learn cycle --frontier google-trends --dry-run` produces ≥ 1 valid promotion proposal with tests, deterministic and reproducible offline from cached data.
- The adoption ledger shows ≥ 20 before/after capability pairs across ≥ 3 topics and 4 languages.
- A regression test proves an adopted amendment changes the answer of a *held-out paraphrase* of the learned class, not just the recorded specific.
- `intent: unknown` rate over the committed trends corpus drops measurably and is ratcheted.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by #656 (E37 promotion protocol — for the apply half; the proposal half can proceed immediately).
- Blocks #657 (E38) and E63-style anticipatory learning.
- Related: #686 (usage-weighted persistence), #687 (meta-request generalization), #674 (E55), #558/#540 (closed parents).

## Process

Collect data to `docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` (frontier census, adoption-rate measurements before/after, online research: Reflexion, Voyager skill libraries, curriculum learning — symbolic analogs only); single PR until every requirement is addressed or explicitly blocked.
title: E59: Close the auto-learning adoption gap — learned items must demonstrably change answers
